articles.description: In a joint statement on Thursday (local time), Bahrain, Jordan, Kuwait, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) signalled their intent to act in 'self-defense in the future'

articles.title: Crowd tries to grab LPG cylinders from moving tempo in Shaikpet
articles.description: Police stepped in to prevent further chaos

articles.title: Kerala polls: Expelled IUML leader Irshad booked over ‘misogynistic’ remarks against CPI(M) legislator U. Prathibha
articles.description: Alex Varghese, District Collector and Election Officer of Alappuzha, conducted a preliminary inquiry into a complaint from the CPI(M) mandalam committee and found a prima facie violation of Model Code of Conduct regarding the remarks

topStories.title: ‘Five Nights at Epstein’s’ Game Goes Viral at US School Campuses
topStories.description: Merve Lapus’s 13-year-old daughter told her dad she was horrified: This month, whenever her teacher stepped out of the room, kids in the class used their devices to play a new online game called Five Nights at Epstein’s.

topStories.title: Amid US-Israel war, Iran football team trains in Turkiye before World Cup
topStories.description: Iran will play two friendly matches in Turkiye as they prepare for the FIFA World Cup in the US, Canada and Mexico.

topStories.title: Transgender persons Amendment Bill opposed by community collectives
topStories.description: The Bill, passed by the Lok Sabha through a voice vote and now pending consideration in the Rajya Sabha, removes the provision for self-identification of gender, a principle recognised by the Supreme Court in the 2014 NALSA judgment and the 2014 Puttaswamy privacy ruling

topStories.title: Campaigners welcome Meta and YouTube's defeat in landmark social media addiction trial
topStories.description: A woman has been awarded $6m in a verdict that could have implications for hundreds of other cases in the US.

topStories.title: ‘Wants to keep India in loop’: Former diplomat Gurjit Singh on Trump's call to PM Modi
topStories.description: Former diplomat Gurjit Singh says the Trump-Modi call signals the US wants to keep India closely involved and consulted in ongoing developments.

topStories.title: Brazil vs France: When and where is the match? Will Mbappe, Vinicius play?
topStories.description: French and Brazilian stars in focus as former title holders meet in a glamour friendly in Boston, US, before World Cup.

topStories.title: Cuban president says Raul Castro involved in US talks amid oil blockade
topStories.description: The talks come amid increasing tensions with the US after Trump said he would be 'honoured' to take over Cuba.

topStories.title: How will the UK respond to US court verdict on social media?
topStories.description: Will the decision of a Los Angeles jury have an impact on the UK government as it considers potential future regulations on social media, writes the BBC's political editor.

topStories.title: Venezuela’s Maduro set to appear in US court months after abduction
topStories.description: The Venezuelan leader, who is accused of plotting to traffic cocaine, denies all charges as part of an imperialist plot.

topStories.title: Helium hitch: Why US-Israel war on Iran could cause MRI scan delays
topStories.description: Shortage of helium will have ripple effects across medical and other industries.

topStories.description: Analysts say a new US disarmament proposal exploits the regional war to enforce inequitable plan for Gaza.

topStories.title: Melania Trump unveils ‘Figure 3’ to 45 nations at White House- Who did humanoid greet in 11 languages, including Bangla
topStories.description: The robot, named Figure 3 welcomed the gathering with a wave and ‘welcome’ in 11 different languages. ‘Swagatam,’ the robot said in what was described as 'Bengali' by a White House pool report.

dailyNews.title: House Democrat bans staff from betting on prediction markets, citing ethics concerns
dailyNews.description: Democratic Rep. Seth Moulton of Massachusetts has prohibited staffers from betting on prediction markets on areas such as politics and any information obtained in an official capacity.

dailyNews.title: Yankees make MLB history as Aaron Judge strikes out four times on Opening Day
dailyNews.description: New York Yankees slugger Aaron Judge had an awful game, but the team was still able to make history in their 7-0 victory over the San Francisco Giants.

dailyNews.title: Princess Beatrice 'distraught’ as Epstein files name her parents, fuel marriage rumors: expert
dailyNews.description: Royal experts say Princess Beatrice is deeply worried about family scandals, torn between loyalty to the former Prince Andrew and her royal family standing.

dailyNews.title: Jayapal doubles down on anti-ICE terror claims as DHS shutdown triggers historic travel chaos
dailyNews.description: Rep. Jayapal says Democrats won't support DHS funding without ICE reforms, as the shutdown hits 40 days and TSA reports record-high airport wait times.

dailyNews.title: More than 90% of Iranian missiles intercepted, but a dangerous imbalance is emerging
dailyNews.description: Experts say Iran's cheap drones and missiles are depleting allied interceptor stockpiles, raising questions about long-term air defense sustainability.

dailyNews.title: MLB Opening Day serves up all-star food lineup that knocks it out of the park
dailyNews.description: With this year's MLB Opening Day here, stadiums are serving wild new eats such as brisket donuts, churro chicken and waffles and a 2-foot loaded hot dog.

dailyNews.title: New York's Hochul drove me to Florida — now she's begging me to return. Not happening
dailyNews.description: Those who could leave New York in the last five years did. The deluge began under Andrew Cuomo and it continues apace under Kathy Hochul

dailyNews.title: America's $39 trillion debt bomb could be more painful than you think
dailyNews.description: With $39 trillion in national debt, politicians are selling myths about growth and tax hikes — but the real risk is to your savings and purchasing power.

topStories.description: The DOJ settled with former Trump national security adviser Michael Flynn for a reported $1.2 million, calling it a step to redress what it described as a "historic injustice."

topStories.title: Trump’s Threats to Europe Put Its Leaders in a Double Bind Over Iran
topStories.description: European politicians risk angering their voters if they join America’s war. Yet they could also face domestic upheaval if they take no action to reopen shipping routes that Iran has blocked and ease an energy crisis.

topStories.description: G.O.P. lawmakers who have given the Trump administration wide latitude to wage war with no congressional input are growing frustrated as officials offer little detail about ground troops, cost or timeline.

dailyNews.title: Some Judges See Risks in Fiery Opinions Warning of Threats to Democracy
dailyNews.description: Federal judges are weighing strategies for how to respond to the high stakes, anonymous threats and politicized atmosphere of the Trump era.

dailyNews.title: Trump Calls for Law Cracking Down on Crime and ‘Rogue Judges’
dailyNews.description: Ahead of the midterms, President Trump told a Republican gathering that he wants to go after repeat offenders and “rogue judges that are criminals.”

dailyNews.title: Trump to Delay Nominating New C.D.C. Director
dailyNews.description: The administration has yet to find a candidate who aligns with Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s agenda while avoiding his unpopular stance on vaccines.

dailyNews.title: Justice Dept. Settles Flynn’s Wrongful Prosecution Suit for $1.25 Million
dailyNews.description: The agreement was an extraordinary example of how the Trump administration has offered legal relief to those aligned with the president.

dailyNews.title: Wealthy Investors Target Foes of Clean Energy, Seeking Revenge
dailyNews.description: Renewable energy leaders said their industry got “rolled” in President Trump’s tax bill. Now they’re fighting back, starting in Texas.

dailyNews.title: How U.S.A.I.D. Birth Control Meant for Africa Was Ruined
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration had options for offloading contraceptives once destined for Africa, a newly obtained memo shows. Instead, it has let them collect dust and go bad.

dailyNews.title: White House turns down Elon Musk's offer to pay TSA workers during DHS shutdown
dailyNews.description: In a post on X Saturday, Musk offered to pay the salaries of TSA workers during the DHS shutdown.

dailyNews.title: White House says Trump will "unleash hell" if Iran doesn't make deal
dailyNews.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt says talks with Tehran are ongoing hours after Iran's state media said the regime rejected Trump administration proposals.

dailyNews.title: Trump official refers New York AG Letitia James for prosecution again
dailyNews.description: A Trump administration official has made new criminal referrals against New York Attorney General Letitia James to federal prosecutors in Miami and Chicago for two cases of possible homeowner's insurance fraud, sources told CBS News.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he doesn't call Iran conflict a "war" due to need for approval from Congress
dailyNews.description: President Trump suggested late Wednesday he's avoiding describing the military conflict with Iran as a "war" because of concerns around the fact that Congress hasn't authorized military force.

dailyNews.title: Former Iran detainees fear Americans held in Iranian prisons face heightened danger
dailyNews.description: Emad Shargi, who was released from Iran's Evin prison in 2023, said "it's important" that President Trump "hears that there are innocent Americans being held like we were as political pawns."

dailyNews.title: 3/25: The Takeout with Major Garrett
dailyNews.description: Long TSA lines continue continue across the country as DHS shutdown hits Day 40; White House says Trump will "unleash Hell" if Iran doesn't make a deal.
